prompt: Using X right now, what AI tools, products, or companies are builders, founders, and engineers most excited about and talking about most in the last 30 days? What is getting real traction (not hype)? Quote specific recent X posts with handles. Cite sources.
content:
**Builders, founders, and engineers on X are buzzing most about agent engineering systems (especially open-source and production-ready ones), AI-native coding/building tools, and a handful of AI infrastructure companies showing real usage and growth.** Hype around one-off chat models has cooled in favor of reusable agents, context tools, safety harnesses, and full-stack builders that ship products. Data comes from recent high-engagement posts (May–early June 2026) discussing GitHub spikes, startup funding/usage reports, and direct recommendations.[[1]](https://x.com/charlie_26c/status/2063516196390285749)[[2]](https://x.com/itsandrewgao/status/2057565907426406719)

### Top Themes with Real Traction
- **Agent engineering & open-source systems**: Focus on reusable, auditable agents with skills, context compression, security/review harnesses, and UI layers rather than demos. Fastest-moving repos show concentrated attention here.
- **AI coding/building stack**: Tools that let non-engineers or small teams ship full apps quickly (Cursor, Lovable, v0, Bolt) combined with Claude for logic/architecture.
- **AI infra with proven momentum**: Companies like Modal, Cerebras, Exa, and Turbopuffer seeing rapid adoption among startups.
- **Emerging platforms**: Continual learning/post-training on product usage data (e.g., Trajectory) and spending/usage reports from a16z highlighting what actual startups pay for.

### Specific Recent X Posts (Quotes with Handles)
Here are direct quotes from notable recent posts by relevant accounts (builders, founders, engineers, or aggregators), focused on the last ~30 days:

- **@charlie_26c** (developer tracking AI products, June 7, 2026): “Today’s AI open-source Top 50 does not look like a new-project spike. It looks like confirmation of a direction: agents are turning from chat boxes into reusable, auditable, product-embedded engineering systems. … ↑ #1 NousResearch/hermes-agent … ↑ #2 anthropics/defending-code-reference-harness … ↑ #16 CopilotKit/CopilotKit … The read: open-source AI is shifting from ‘show the model’ to ‘ship the agent system.’” (Highlights daily GitHub movement around agent infrastructure.)[[1]](https://x.com/charlie_26c/status/2063516196390285749)

- **@itsandrewgao** (ex-Sequoia/LangChain/Cognition, May 21, 2026): “ai infra startups have been killing it lately: modal cerebras exa turbopuffer (all within the last week!)” (Direct callout of infra traction among technical users.)[[2]](https://x.com/itsandrewgao/status/2057565907426406719)

- **@madebydanl** (product builder, June 1, 2026): “Tools driving this movement: → @cursor_ai: AI-native code editor → @Lovable: build full-stack apps by chatting → @v0 by Vercel — UI generation from prompts → @bolt: instant web apps → @claudeai: logic, debugging, architecture.” (Practical stack recommendation for builders.)[[3]](https://x.com/madebydanl/status/2061526222388027876)

- **@rronak_** (co-founder of Trajectory, ex-Google DeepMind/Stanford, May 27, 2026): “Today, @MichaelElabd, @QuantumArjun, and I are excited to announce Trajectory. We are a research lab and product company building the platform for Continual Learning. … We’ve raised $15M from @Conviction, @BessemerVP … We’re partnering with some of the best AI-native companies: @ClayRunHQ @Harvey, @DecagonAI, @mercor_ai, @RogoAI to power their agentic systems, some of which we are already in production with.” (New platform with real customer traction in agentic systems.)[[4]](https://x.com/rronak_/status/2059644771262730624)

- **@sandy_carter** (AI/Web3 voice, June 7, 2026): “BREAKING: a16z just released the 50 AI tools startups spend the most money on … A few patterns stand out: •OpenAI and Anthropic dominate the stack •Cursor, Replit, and Lovable are making every team more technical •Clay and Instantly are becoming standard for outbound •Voice AI is everywhere (ElevenLabs, Otter, Krisp, Plaud) •AI meeting assistants are turning conversations into searchable company knowledge.” (Spending data as a proxy for real usage.)[[5]](https://x.com/sandy_carter/status/2063429812526669895)

- **@gusik4ever** (May 30, 2026): Shared a list of fastest-growing GitHub repos in finance + AI, including “1. TradingAgents (+~2,000 ★) multi-agent LLM trading framework … 2. MoneyPrinterTurbo (+11,147 ★) one-click short video generator … 3. OpenBB (+~1,500 ★) open-source financial data platform …” (Concrete GitHub traction signals in applied AI.)[[6]](https://x.com/gusik4ever/status/2060693851598107006)

Other mentions include YC-backed legal AI (e.g., Legora hitting high ARR quickly) and various agent-building resources, but the above cluster around repeated themes of production agents, coding tools, and infra with measurable signals (stars, funding, partnerships, a16z spend data).[[7]](https://x.com/ycombinator/status/2056817226330845482)

These reflect discussions among technical builders rather than pure consumer hype. Open-source agent tooling and the Cursor/Lovable-style builder stack appear to have the broadest, most consistent engagement from engineers and founders.
